channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Black Hat USA
The Context Gap: How Nearly Half of Your Time is Lost to Investigation | UpGuard
item.description: Security teams waste 43% of their response time on manual context gathering, making high-context intelligence a necessity in modern cybersecurity.

item.title: DPRK-Linked Hackers Use GitHub as C2 in Multi-Stage Attacks Targeting South Korea
item.description: Threat&nbsp;actors likely associated with the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) have been observed using GitHub as command-and-control (C2) infrastructure in multi-stage attacks targeting organizations in South&nbsp;Korea. The&nbsp;attack chain,&nbsp;per Fortinet FortiGuard&nbsp;Labs, involves obfuscated Windows shortcut (LNK) files acting as the starting point to drop a decoy PDF
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Fortinet Flaw Allows Unauthorized Access to Enterprise Systems
item.description: <p>Fortinet warns of a critical FortiClient EMS zero-day vulnerability that is currently being exploited, allowing attackers to bypass authentication and execute commands.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fortinet-forticlient-ems-zero-day-active-exploitation/">New Fortinet Flaw Allows Unauthorized Access to Enterprise Systems</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
item.description: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Apple Rolls Out Fix: New macOS Update Could Protect 100M Mac Users
item.description: <p>Apple’s macOS 26.4 update adds a Terminal warning to help stop ClickFix-style attacks by flagging potentially harmful pasted commands.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-macos-update-terminal-malware-warning-clickfix/">Apple Rolls Out Fix: New macOS Update Could Protect 100M Mac Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Shadow AI in Healthcare Is Here to Stay
item.description: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
item.description: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards groups recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps
item.description: Your attack surface no&nbsp;longer lives&nbsp;on one operating system, and neither do the campaigns targeting&nbsp;it. In enterprise environments, attackers move across Windows endpoints, executive MacBooks, Linux infrastructure, and mobile devices, taking advantage of the fact that many SOC&nbsp;workflows are still fragmented by&nbsp;platform.&nbsp; For security leaders, this creates&nbsp;a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Axios Hack, Chrome 0-Day, Fortinet Exploits, Paragon Spyware and More
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How LiteLLM Turned Developer Machines Into Credential Vaults for Attackers
item.description: The&nbsp;most active piece of enterprise infrastructure in the company is the developer workstation. That&nbsp;laptop is where credentials are created, tested, cached, copied, and reused across services, bots, build tools, and now local AI&nbsp;agents. In&nbsp;March 2026, the TeamPCP threat&nbsp;actor proved just how&nbsp;valuable developer&nbsp;machines are. Their&nbsp;supply chain attack on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Wants to Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography by 2029
item.description: <p>Google <a href="https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/safety-security/cryptography-migration-timeline/">says</a> that it will fully transition to post-quantum cryptography by 2029. I think this is a good move, not because I think we will have a useful quantum computer anywhere near that year, but because crypto-agility is always a good thing.</p> <p>Slashdot <a href="https://it.slashdot.org/story/26/03/27/2123239/google-moves-post-quantum-encryption-timeline-up-to-2029">thread</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools
item.description: Threat actors associated&nbsp;with Qilin&nbsp;and Warlock ransomware operations&nbsp;have been&nbsp;observed using the bring your own vulnerable driver&nbsp;(BYOVD) technique to silence security tools running on compromised hosts, according to findings from Cisco Talos and Trend&nbsp;Micro. Qilin attacks analyzed by Talos&nbsp;have been&nbsp;found to deploy a malicious DLL named "msimg32.dll,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: BKA Identifies REvil Leaders Behind 130 German Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (aka BKA or the Bundeskriminalamt) has unmasked the real identities of two of the key figures associated with the now-defunct REvil (aka Sodinokibi) ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation. One of the threat actors, who went by the alias UNKN, functioned as a representative of the group, advertising the ransomware in June 2019 on the XSS cybercrime forum
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: $285 Million Drift Hack Traced to Six-Month DPRK Social Engineering Operation
item.description: Drift&nbsp;has revealed that the April 1, 2026, attack that led to&nbsp;the theft of $285&nbsp;million was the culmination of a months-long targeted and meticulously planned social engineering operation undertaken by the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) that began in the fall of&nbsp;2025. The&nbsp;Solana-based decentralized exchange described it as "an attack six months in the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy reverse shells, harvest credentials, and drop a persistent&nbsp;implant. "Every package contains three files (package.json, index.js, postinstall.js), has no description, repository,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS
item.description: Fortinet has released out-of-band patches for a critical security flaw impacting FortiClient EMS that it said has been exploited in the&nbsp;wild. The&nbsp;vulnerability, tracked&nbsp;as CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS score: 9.1), has been described as a pre-authentication API access bypass leading to privilege escalation. "An improper access control vulnerability [CWE-284] in FortiClient EMS may allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jurassic Fish Chokes on Squid
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Inconsistent Privacy Labels Don't Tell Users What They Are Getting
item.description: Data privacy labels are a great idea for mobile apps, but the current versions just aren't good enough.
item.dc:creator: Bree Fowler

item.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
item.description: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025,&nbsp;following a two-year&nbsp;period of minimal targeting in the&nbsp;region. The campaign has been attributed&nbsp;to TA416, a cluster of activity that overlaps with DarkPeony, RedDelta, Red Lich, SmugX, UNC6384, and Vertigo&nbsp;Panda. "This TA416 activity included multiple
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple Breaks Precedent, Patches DarkSword for iOS 18
item.description: Even organizations with users unwilling or unable to adopt iOS 26 can now protect themselves from a severe mobile OS-cracking tool.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Android Alert: 50 Google Play Apps Linked to ‘NoVoice’ Malware Reached 2.3M Downloads
item.description: <p>NoVoice malware was found in 50 Android apps on Google Play, with 2.3 million downloads, by bypassing detection and targeting outdated devices.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-novoice-malware-android-google-play-50-apps/">Android Alert: 50 Google Play Apps Linked to ‘NoVoice’ Malware Reached 2.3M Downloads</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: FBI Declares Surveillance System Breach a ‘Major Incident’
item.description: <p>China-linked hackers breached an FBI surveillance system, exposing sensitive investigation data and prompting a “major incident” classification.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-china-linked-fbi-surveillance-breach-major-incident/">FBI Declares Surveillance System Breach a ‘Major Incident’</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Microsoft Details Cookie-Controlled PHP Web Shells Persisting via Cron on Linux Servers
item.description: Threat&nbsp;actors are increasingly using HTTP cookies as a control channel for PHP-based web shells on Linux servers and to achieve remote code execution, according to findings from the Microsoft Defender Security Research&nbsp;Team. "Instead of exposing command execution through URL parameters or request bodies, these web shells rely on threat actor-supplied cookie values to gate execution,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Toast, Inc.
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
item.description: As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: AI Breakthroughs, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from March 30–April 3.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-breakthroughs-security-breaches-and-industry-shakeups-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Breakthroughs, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Picking Up 'Skull Vibrations'? Could Be XR Headset Authentication
item.description: &quot;Skull vibration harmonics generated by vital signs&quot; can be used to sign in to VR, AR, and MR headsets, according to emerging research.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
item.description: Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Chainguard Unveils Factory 2.0 to Automate Hardening the Software Supply Chain
item.description: The rebuilt Chainguard platform adds deeper security designed to continuously reconcile open source artifacts across containers, libraries, agent skills, and GitHub Actions.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM Can Now Ingest Microsoft Defender Telemetry
item.description: Once CrowdStrike's nemesis, Microsoft is now a collaborator. A shared interest in Formula 1 helped thaw the years-long fierce rivalry.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: Company that Secretly Records and Publishes Zoom Meetings
item.description: <p>WebinarTV searches the internet for public Zoom invites, joins the meetings, secretly records them, and <a href="https://www.404media.co/this-company-is-secretly-turning-your-zoom-calls-into-ai-podcasts/">publishes</a> (alternate <a href="https://archive.ph/TYd4L">link</a>) the recordings. It doesn&#8217;t use the Zoom record feature, so Zoom can&#8217;t do anything about it.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: UNC1069 Social Engineering of Axios Maintainer Led to npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The&nbsp;maintainer of the Axios npm package has confirmed that the supply chain compromise was the result of a highly-targeted social engineering campaign orchestrated by North Korean threat actors tracked&nbsp;as UNC1069. Maintainer Jason Saayman said the attackers tailored their social engineering efforts "specifically to me" by first approaching him under the guise of the founder of a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture
item.description: The next major&nbsp;breach hitting your clients probably won't come&nbsp;from inside their&nbsp;walls. It'll come through a vendor they trust, a SaaS tool their finance team signed up for, or a subcontractor nobody in IT knows about. That's the new attack surface, and most organizations are underprepared for&nbsp;it. Cynomi's new&nbsp;guide, Securing the Modern Perimeter: The Rise of Third-Party
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New SparkCat Variant in iOS, Android Apps Steals Crypto Wallet Recovery Phrase Images
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers&nbsp;have discovered a new version of&nbsp;the SparkCat malware on the Apple App Store and Google Play Store, more than a year after the&nbsp;trojan was discovered targeting both the mobile operating&nbsp;systems. The&nbsp;malware&nbsp;has&nbsp;been found to conceal itself within seemingly benign apps, such as enterprise messengers and food delivery services, while
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Drift Loses $285 Million in Durable Nonce Social Engineering Attack Linked to DPRK
item.description: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1,&nbsp;2026. "Earlier today, a malicious actor gained unauthorized access to Drift Protocol through a novel attack involving durable nonces, resulting in a rapid takeover of Drift’s Security Council administrative powers," the&
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Phishing Platform Used in Credential Theft Campaigns Against C-Suite Execs
item.description: A large-scale credential theft campaign targeting senior executives has been linked to a previously unknown automated phishing platform called Venom

item.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
item.description: AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bracken, Kristina Beek

item.title: Not Toying Around: Hasbro Attack May Take 'Weeks' to Remediate
item.description: The company's 8-K filing notes &quot;unauthorized access&quot; and that it has activated business continuity plans and taken some systems offline.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A&nbsp;large-scale credential harvesting operation&nbsp;has been&nbsp;observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Services (AWS) secrets, shell command history, Stripe API keys, and GitHub tokens at&nbsp;scale. Cisco&nbsp;Talos has attributed the operation to a threat cluster it tracks&nbsp;as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Security Bosses Are All-In on AI. Here's Why
item.description: CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its future promise.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: Patch Now: Chrome Flaw Under Active Attack, Google Confirms
item.description: <p>Google patches 21 Chrome vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw that could enable code execution and full device compromise.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chrome-zero-day-cve-2026-5281-active-exploit/">Patch Now: Chrome Flaw Under Active Attack, Google Confirms</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: US Bans All Foreign-Made Consumer Routers
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Hasbro Cyberattack Knocks Systems Offline, Recovery Could Take Weeks
item.description: <p>Hasbro is investigating a cyberattack that forced systems offline, warning recovery could take weeks as it works to contain the incident and assess the impact.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-hasbro-cyberattack-systems-offline-weeks-recovery/">Hasbro Cyberattack Knocks Systems Offline, Recovery Could Take Weeks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Apple Issues Rare Patch: Up to 270M iPhones Could Be Vulnerable to ‘DarkSword’ Exploit
item.description: <p>Apple issues a rare iOS 18 security patch as the DarkSword exploit threatens up to 270 million iPhones, marking a shift in its long-standing update policy.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-ios-18-darksword-exploit-security-patch/">Apple Issues Rare Patch: Up to 270M iPhones Could Be Vulnerable to ‘DarkSword’ Exploit</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Madeline Clarke

item.title: RSAC 2026: AI Dominates, But Community Remains Key to Security
item.description: As AI took center stage at this year's conference, experts debated automation, oversight and the evolving role of human intelligence in cybersecurity — despite the US government's notable absence.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Beek, Rob Wright

item.title: Cisco Patches 9.8 CVSS IMC and SSM Flaws Allowing Remote System Compromise
item.description: Cisco&nbsp;has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and gain access to the system with elevated privileges. The&nbsp;vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20093, carries a CVSS score of 9.8&nbsp;out of a maximum of&nbsp;10.0. "This
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New 'Storm' Infostealer Remotely Decrypts Stolen Credentials
item.description: This modern infostealer adopted server-side decryption of stolen credentials to bypass security controls

item.title: NCSC Issues Security Alert Over Hackers Targeting WhatsApp and Signal Accounts
item.description: The UK’s cybersecurity agency offered advice to “high-risk’ individuals” on how to protect against social engineering and cyber-attacks

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18 Security Updates Amid DarkSword Threat
item.description: iOS/iPadOS 18.7.7 updates expanded to protect older devices from DarkSword web exploit kit

item.title: Bank Trojan 'Casbaneiro' Worms Through Latin America
item.description: Augmented Marauder's multipronged banking-Trojan cyber campaigns are targeting Spanish speakers, evading detection, and replicating rapidly.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Researchers Observe Sub-One-Hour Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Halcyon says Akira is now capable of carrying out an entire ransomware attack in less than an hour

item.title: GitHub Used as Covert Channel in Multi-Stage Malware Campaign
item.description: LNK files use GitHub C2, embedded decoders and PowerShell for persistence and data exfiltration

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Pre-Auth Chains, Android Rootkits, CloudTrail Evasion & 10 More Stories
item.description: The&nbsp;latest ThreatsDay Bulletin is basically a cheat sheet for everything breaking on the internet right now. No&nbsp;corporate fluff or boring lectures here, just a quick and honest look at the messy reality of keeping systems safe this&nbsp;week. Things&nbsp;are moving fast. The&nbsp;list includes researchers chaining small bugs together to create massive backdoors, old software flaws
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
item.description: A&nbsp;financially motivated operation&nbsp;codenamed REF1695&nbsp;has been&nbsp;observed leveraging fake installers to deploy remote access trojans (RATs) and cryptocurrency miners since November&nbsp;2023. "Beyond cryptomining, the threat actor monetizes infections through CPA (Cost Per Action) fraud, directing victims to content locker pages under the guise of software registration," Elastic
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
item.description: In December&nbsp;2025, we shared the first-ever The State of Trusted Open&nbsp;Source report, featuring insights from our product data and customer base on open source consumption across our catalog of container image projects, versions, images, language libraries, and builds. These&nbsp;insights shed light on what teams pull, deploy, and maintain day to day, alongside the vulnerabilities and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Possible US Government iPhone Hacking Tool Leaked
item.description: <p>Wired <a href="https://www.wired.com/story/coruna-iphone-hacking-toolkit-us-government/">writes</a> (alternate <a href="https://archive.ph/vN8U5">source</a>):</p> <blockquote><p>Security researchers at Google on Tuesday <a href="https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/coruna-powerful-ios-exploit-kit">released a report</a> describing what they&#8217;re calling &#8220;Coruna,&#8221; a highly sophisticated iPhone hacking toolkit that includes five complete hacking techniques capable of bypassing all the defenses of an iPhone to silently install malware on a device when it visits a website containing the exploitation code. In total, Coruna takes advantage of 23 distinct vulnerabilities in iOS, a rare collection of hacking components that suggests it was created by a well-resourced, likely state-sponsored group of hackers...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: WhatsApp Alerts 200 Users After Fake iOS App Installed Spyware; Italian Firm Faces Action
item.description: Meta-owned messaging platform WhatsApp said it alerted about 200 users who were tricked into installing a bogus version of its iOS app that was infected with&nbsp;spyware. According to reports from Italian&nbsp;newspaper La Repubblica and news&nbsp;agency ANSA, the vast majority of the targets are located in Italy. It's assessed that the threat actors behind the activity used social engineering
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most CNI Firms Face Up to £5m in Downtime from OT Attacks
item.description: E2e-assure says 80% of critical infrastructure providers could face millions in downtime from cyber-attacks

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18.7.7 Update to More Devices to Block DarkSword Exploit
item.description: Apple&nbsp;on&nbsp;Wednesday expanded the availability of iOS 18.7.7&nbsp;and iPadOS 18.7.7&nbsp;to a broader range of devices to protect users from the risk posed by a recently disclosed exploit kit known&nbsp;as DarkSword. "We enabled the availability of iOS 18.7.7&nbsp;for more devices on April 1, 2026, so users with Automatic Updates turned on can automatically receive important security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ransomware Will Hit Hospitals. Rehearsals Are Key to Defense
item.description: A chief medical information officer describes what hospitals face when they inevitably suffer a ransomware attack—whether it leads to short- or long-term outages.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: LatAm's Self-Taught Cyber Talent Overlooked Amid Cyberattack Glut
item.description: A newly released study exclusively shared with Dark Reading details the unique circumstances that make up Latin America's labor pool, and why organizations may want to expand their talent search.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Is “Hackback” Official US Cybersecurity Strategy?
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cyberattacks Intensify Pressure on Latin American Governments
item.description: Cyber threats across Latin America are increasingly targeting government systems, from disruptive attacks in Puerto Rico to a surge of probes against Colombia’s health sector.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: CERT-UA Impersonation Campaign Spread AGEWHEEZE Malware to 1 Million Emails
item.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new phishing campaign in which the cybersecurity agency itself was impersonated to distribute a remote administration tool known as AGEWHEEZE. As part of the attacks, the threat actors, tracked as UAC-0255, sent emails on March 26 and 27, 2026, posing as CERT-UA to distribute a password-protected ZIP archive
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: A Panel Discussion on Cryptography
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Microsoft: Hackers Are Using WhatsApp to Deliver Malware to Windows PCs
item.description: <p>Hackers are using WhatsApp messages to deliver malware to Windows PCs, exploiting user trust and attachments to trigger stealthy, multi-stage attacks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-whatsapp-malware-windows-attack/">Microsoft: Hackers Are Using WhatsApp to Deliver Malware to Windows PCs</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Google Introduces Android Dev Verification Amid Openness Debate
item.description: Android requires dev identity verification for sideloaded apps; phased global rollout from September

item.title: Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Commoditizes ClickFix Attacks
item.description: A new service on the cybercrime market provides automated capabilities to create persistent information-stealing social engineering attacks.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp-Delivered VBS Malware Hijacking Windows via UAC Bypass
item.description: Microsoft is calling attention to a new campaign that has leveraged WhatsApp messages to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBS) files. The activity, beginning in late February 2026, leverages these scripts to initiate a multi-stage infection chain for establishing persistence and enabling remote access. It's currently not known what lures the threat actors use to trick users into
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Warns Quantum Computers Could Crack Crypto Sooner Than Expected
item.description: <p>Google warns that quantum computers could break crypto sooner than expected, heightening the urgency for post-quantum security across blockchain networks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-quantum-computing-crypto-security-risk/">Google Warns Quantum Computers Could Crack Crypto Sooner Than Expected</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Madeline Clarke

item.title: New Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Automates Continuous Data Theft
item.description: Venom Stealer malware-as-a-service automates ClickFix social engineering, credential and crypto exfiltration

item.title: Block the Prompt, Not the Work: The End of "Doctor No"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Casbaneiro Phishing Targets Latin America and Europe Using Dynamic PDF Lures
item.description: A multi-pronged phishing campaign is targeting Spanish-speaking users in organizations across Latin America and Europe to deliver Windows banking trojans like Casbaneiro (aka Metamorfo) via another malware called Horabot. The activity has been attributed to a Brazilian cybercrime threat actor tracked as Augmented Marauder and Water Saci. The e-crime group was first documented by Trend Micro in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target European Governments in Espionage Campaigns
item.description: Chinese state-backed group TA416 had suspended its cyber espionage operations in Europe since 2023, noted Proofpoint

item.title: New Chrome Zero-Day CVE-2026-5281 Under Active Exploitation — Patch Released
item.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address 21 vulnerabilities, including a zero-day flaw that it said has been exploited in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-5281 (CVSS score: N/A), concerns a use-after-free bug in Dawn, an open-source and cross-platform implementation of the WebGPU standard. "Use-after-free in Dawn in Google Chrome prior
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Security Risks: Proofpoint CSO Ryan Kalember, Live at RSAC 2026
3 Reasons Attackers Are Using Your Trusted Tools Against You (And Why You Don’t See It Coming)
item.description: For years, cybersecurity has followed a familiar model: block malware, stop the attack. Now, attackers are moving on to what’s next. Threat actors now use malware less frequently in favor of what’s already inside your environment, including abusing trusted tools, native binaries, and legitimate admin utilities to move laterally, escalate privileges, and persist without raising alarms. Most
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Are We Training AI Too Late?
item.description: Ask the Expert: Cybersecurity teams need to expand their field of view to include new, unique threat sources, rather than relying on past, proven threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Nishawn Smagh

item.title: A Taxonomy of Cognitive Security
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Eight in 10 UK Manufacturers Hit by Cyber Incident in a Year
item.description: Most UK manufacturers compromised last year suffered financial loss, says ESET

item.title: Digital assets after death: Managing risks to your loved one’s digital estate
item.description: Fraudsters often target the accounts of the deceased or their grieving relatives. Here’s how to keep the scammers at bay.

item.title: Hackers Hijack Axios npm Package to Spread RATs
item.description: Threat actors hijacked the popular npm package axios to spread RAT malware after compromising an open‑source maintainer’s account, researchers warn

item.title: Google Attributes Axios npm Supply Chain Attack to North Korean Group UNC1069
item.description: Google has formally attributed the supply chain compromise of the popular Axios npm package to a financially motivated North Korean threat activity cluster tracked as UNC1069. "We have attributed the attack to a suspected North Korean threat actor we track as UNC1069," John Hultquist, chief analyst at Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), told The Hacker News in a statement. "North Korean
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Claude Code Source Leaked via npm Packaging Error, Anthropic Confirms
item.description: Anthropic on Tuesday confirmed that internal code for its popular artificial intelligence (AI) coding assistant, Claude Code, had been inadvertently released due to a human error. "No sensitive customer data or credentials were involved or exposed," an Anthropic spokesperson said in a statement shared with CNBC News. "This was a release packaging issue caused by human error, not a security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Forgotten Endpoint: Security Risks of Dormant Devices
item.description: Technology Talk: That forgotten notebook holds plenty of secrets to enterprise access.
item.dc:creator: Abib Oyebamiji

item.title: New North Korean AI Hiring Scheme Targets US Companies
item.description: <p>North Korean operatives are using AI-generated resumes and stolen identities to infiltrate US companies, turning hiring pipelines into a new attack vector.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-north-korean-ai-hiring-scheme-us-companies/">New North Korean AI Hiring Scheme Targets US Companies</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Axios NPM Package Compromised in Precision Attack
item.description: The NPM package for Axios, a popular JavaScript HTTP client library, was briefly compromised this week, possibly by North Korean threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Google's Vertex AI Is Over-Privileged. That's a Problem
item.description: Palo Alto Networks researchers show how attackers could exploit AI agents on Google's Vertex AI to steal data and break into restricted cloud infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: TeamPCP Breaches Cloud, SaaS Instances With Stolen Credentials
item.description: The threat group's shift to speedy attacks on AWS, Azure, and SaaS instances shows organizations need to respond quickly to compromised credentials.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Android Developer Verification Rollout Begins Ahead of September Enforcement
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Drive Expands AI Ransomware Detection, File Recovery to More Users
item.description: <p>Google expands Drive ransomware detection and file recovery with its latest AI model, which detects 14 times more infections as the features move beyond beta.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-drive-ransomware-detection-file-recovery/">Google Drive Expands AI Ransomware Detection, File Recovery to More Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in the TrueConf client video conferencing software has been exploited in the wild as a zero-day as part of a campaign targeting government entities in Southeast Asia dubbed TrueChaos. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-3502 (CVSS score: 7.8), a lack of integrity check when fetching application update code, allowing an attacker to distribute a tampered update,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Maryland Man Charged Over $53m Uranium Finance Crypto Hack
item.description: Maryland man accused of $53m Uranium Finance hack, exploited smart contract flaws, laundered funds

item.title: AI and Quantum Are Forcing a Rethink of Digital Trust
item.description: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, DigiCert CEO Amit Sinha explains how AI-driven identities and quantum threats are reshaping the foundations of digital trust.
item.dc:creator: Terry Sweeney

item.title: Phantom Project Bundles Infostealer, Crypter and RAT For Sale
item.description: Phantom Stealer .NET harvests browser credentials, cookies, cards, sessions, as stealer-as-a-service

item.title: Iran Deploys 'Pseudo-Ransomware,' Revives Pay2Key Operations
item.description: Iranian APTs are blurring the lines between state-sponsored and cybercriminal activities to target high-impact US organizations.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security "blind spot" in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform that could allow artificial intelligence (AI) agents to be weaponized by an attacker to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data and compromise an organization's cloud environment. According to Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the issue relates to how the Vertex AI permission model can be misused
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ChatGPT Security Issue Enabled Data Theft via Single Prompt
item.description: OpenAI has patched vulnerability, which Check Point said was because of a DNS loophole
